message:
ci: cross-platform matrix + feature matrix on lint+test jobs (br-0l5r + br-kmbc)

Slots two matrix strategies onto the ffhs-split CI jobs that were
ready for them.

br-0l5r (HIGH): test + lint now run on [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
  - `test` matrix: 2 OS × 1 feature set = 2 cells
  - Verifies the cross-platform claim (path separators, line endings,
    calamine Excel zip handling) on the two POSIX platforms.
  - Windows is deferred per the bead's Phase 1 recommendation —
    many existing tests assume POSIX behavior; Windows is a
    separate follow-on bead once 0l5r's POSIX matrix is green.

br-kmbc (HIGH): lint gains a `features` matrix dimension
  - "" (default)
  - "--no-default-features"
  - "--all-features" (activates asupersync on fp-runtime +
    fp-conformance; future fd90 slices 2-3 SQL backend features
    will land here automatically)
  - Applied to cargo check + cargo clippy in lint.
  - Rustdoc runs only on the --all-features cell (doc the full
    surface; no need to re-render docs 3 times).
  - lint matrix: 2 OS × 3 feature sets = 6 cells total.

Both matrices use `fail-fast: false` so one cell failing doesn't
cancel the others. Reviewers see per-cell status — e.g. "lint
(macos-latest, --no-default-features)" as a distinct green/red
badge.

Preserved unchanged:
  - fmt job (formatter is deterministic across OS; 1 cell suffices).
  - conformance + gates jobs (ubuntu-only; pandas oracle install
    is heavy and live-oracle tests are the fixed point — running
    them 2-3x wastes runner time).
  - security + licenses + secret-scan + fuzz-regression (already
    ubuntu-only per ffhs refactor).

CI cost estimate:
  Before 0l5r+kmbc: lint(1) + test(1) = 2 cells.
  After: lint(6) + test(2) = 8 cells. +6 cells.
  Runner-minute increase ≈ 30-60 min per PR total (matrix jobs
  run in parallel, so wall-clock grows only by the slower runner
  (macos ≈ 1.5-2x ubuntu); critical-path time grows by maybe
  2-3 min).

Expected teething:
  - First macos runs may surface hard-coded "/tmp" paths or
    LF-assumes; those get fixed as per-test bugs or cfg-gated.
  - First --no-default-features run may flag a feature-gated
    dep that wasn't properly behind `#[cfg(feature = "X")]`;
    those get fixed as feature-hygiene bugs (tracked under
    br-tne4's config-struct sweep follow-on).

YAML validated via yaml.safe_load.

Related:
- br-ffhs (CI job split, shipped) — prerequisite. Without the
  split, adding a matrix to the old mega-job would have made
  it 6-18x more cells with 7-min wall-clock each. The split
  makes the matrix tractable.
- br-tne4 (#[non_exhaustive], shipped) — --no-default-features
  cell will surface any config-struct missing public
  reachability with default features off.
- br-0iyb (concurrency group, shipped) — canceling stale matrix
  cells on PR-push saves 30-60 cell-minutes per superseded push.
- br-fd90 (SQL backend epic) — slices 2-3 will add sql-sqlite /
  sql-postgresql / sql-mysql features. kmbc's feature matrix
  will automatically exercise each.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,7 +39,15 @@ jobs:
         run: cargo fmt --check
 
   lint:
-    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
+        features:
+          - ""                        # default features
+          - "--no-default-features"   # minimum-viable build
+          - "--all-features"          # every feature on (asupersync etc.)
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
     env:
       CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
       CARGO_TARGET_DIR: ${{ github.workspace }}/.target-lint
@@ -65,18 +73,23 @@ jobs:
           components: clippy, rust-src
 
       - name: Check
-        run: cargo check --all-targets
+        run: cargo check --all-targets ${{ matrix.features }}
 
       - name: Clippy
-        run: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets ${{ matrix.features }} -- -D warnings
 
       - name: Rustdoc
+        if: matrix.features == '--all-features'
         env:
           RUSTDOCFLAGS: -D warnings
         run: cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --all-features
 
   test:
-    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
     env:
       CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
       CARGO_TARGET_DIR: ${{ github.workspace }}/.target
